cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The ideological, political, and military rivalry between the United States and Soviet Union (1947–1991) without direct armed conflict between the two superpowers

cards.prompt: The US foreign policy strategy of preventing Soviet communism from spreading to new countries

cards.prompt: Churchill's Iron Curtain speech — symbolic beginning of the Cold War

cards.prompt: The military alliance of Western democratic nations formed in 1949 to defend against Soviet expansion

cards.prompt: The Soviet-led military alliance formed in 1955 in response to NATO, including Eastern European satellite states

cards.prompt: The 1961 construction by East Germany of a barrier between East and West Berlin to prevent defection to the West

cards.prompt: Cuban Missile Crisis — the most dangerous moment of the Cold War

cards.prompt: The US-Soviet space and technology competition symbolized by Sputnik, the Moon landing, and nuclear weapons development

cards.prompt: Soviet Union launches Sputnik, first artificial satellite

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The US program to rebuild Western Europe economically after WWII, intended to prevent the spread of communism to impoverished nations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The process by which Asian and African nations gained independence from European colonial powers, primarily 1945–1975

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mohandas Gandhi
cards.answer: Indian independence leader who used nonviolent civil disobedience (satyagraha) to resist British rule and lead India to independence in 1947

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Indian independence from Britain

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The partition of British India into two independent nations along religious lines — Hindu-majority India and Muslim-majority Pakistan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Kwame Nkrumah
cards.answer: First president of Ghana (1957) who led the first sub-Saharan African nation to achieve independence and became a leading voice for Pan-Africanism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The political ideology advocating African political unity and solidarity, associated with leaders like Nkrumah and Haile Selassie

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ho Chi Minh
cards.answer: Vietnamese communist leader who led resistance against French colonialism and American intervention, eventually unifying Vietnam under communism in 1975

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1954–1975 conflict in which the US intervened to prevent the communist takeover of South Vietnam, ultimately failing

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1950–1953 conflict on the Korean Peninsula in which US-led UN forces halted North Korea's communist invasion of South Korea

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The South African system of institutionalized racial segregation and white minority rule (1948–1994)

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Nelson Mandela
cards.answer: South African anti-apartheid leader who was imprisoned for 27 years, became the first Black president of South Africa (1994), and led the transition to democracy
alternate_answers: First Black president of South Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The geopolitical strategy of the Soviet Union to maintain control over Eastern European communist regimes as buffer states against the West

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Fall of the Berlin Wall — symbolic end of the Cold War

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Soviet Union officially dissolves

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Nations that refused to align with either the US or Soviet blocs during the Cold War, often newly decolonized nations

cards.prompt: India and Pakistan gain independence from Britain

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Korean War (proxy conflict between US and Soviet-backed forces)

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Cuban Missile Crisis — closest the Cold War came to nuclear war

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Vietnam War ends with US withdrawal and North Vietnamese victory

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Soviet Union collapses, ending the Cold War

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The US foreign policy doctrine (1947) committing to containing the spread of Soviet communism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The US economic aid program that provided billions to rebuild Western European economies after World War II, also aimed at preventing communist revolutions

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The military alliance of Western nations formed in 1949 to provide collective defense against Soviet aggression

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Soviet-led military alliance of Eastern European communist states formed in 1955 in response to NATO

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Jawaharlal Nehru
cards.answer: India's first prime minister after independence (1947); co-founder of the Non-Aligned Movement, which allowed newly independent nations to avoid commitment to either superpower

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The movement of newly independent nations that rejected alignment with either the US-led West or Soviet-led East during the Cold War

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ho Chi Minh
cards.answer: Vietnamese communist leader who led the struggle for independence from France and then against US-backed South Vietnam, resulting in unified communist Vietnam in 1975

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Kwame Nkrumah
cards.answer: First prime minister and president of independent Ghana (1957), pan-African leader who advocated for African unity and became a symbol of decolonization across Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The system of institutionalized racial segregation in South Africa (1948–1994) that denied rights to Black South Africans

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Nelson Mandela
cards.answer: South African anti-apartheid leader imprisoned for 27 years who became the country's first Black president (1994) after the end of apartheid

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Berlin Wall is built, dividing East and West Germany

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Berlin Wall falls, symbolizing the end of the Cold War

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The policy of nuclear weapons possessed by both superpowers ensuring that any nuclear attack would result in the complete destruction of both sides

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The wave of African independence movements in the 1960s when over 17 African countries gained independence, primarily from France

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Wars in which the US and USSR supported opposing sides without directly fighting each other, including Korea, Vietnam, Angola, and Afghanistan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mao Zedong's radical campaign to rapidly industrialize China through collective farming and backyard steel production that caused 15–45 million deaths by famine

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mao's campaign against traditional and capitalist elements in Chinese society (1966–1976) that disrupted education, culture, and the economy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Soviet leader who denounced Stalin's cult of personality and pursued peaceful coexistence with the West during the 1950s–60s

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The process by which European powers granted independence to their African and Asian colonies, primarily in the 1950s–60s

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mohandas Gandhi
cards.answer: Indian independence leader who used nonviolent civil disobedience (satyagraha) to resist British colonial rule, leading India to independence in 1947 and inspiring civil rights movements worldwide
